body_txt: Sugar and sweetness are culturally coded as childish and feminine, while any other flavor is mature and manly. Boys may scorn sweets in public to be accepted as a man among men , and preferring your coffee black is a mark of adulthood for many. Complementing this is the stereotype that women do like sweets and chocolate in particular, and the trope in which a heartbroken young woman turns to ice cream is well-known. This cultural phenomenon is common in (but not specific to) Japan. If a Japanese male character's love of sweets is portrayed as a positive trait, it may be to make him seem cute and innocent , and In Touch with His Feminine Side . But even in Japan, this is only true in some social contexts, but it is generally assumed that women like their sweet stuff sweeter and more elaborate. In Western contexts, this stereotype only applies in regards to alcoholic beverages: "real men" drink cordials and sweet liqueurs only occasionally if at all, avoid fruit mixes (except perhaps fresh lemon or lime, or "classic" mixes involving fresh fruit juice), and never consume any mass-marketed sweetened alcoholic drinks (i.e, wine coolers, alcopops, and other "girly drinks"); no, a real man relishes in the harshness and bitterness of his whisk(e)y/brandy/rum/vodka/gin/tequila and especially beer. Less often, coffee and tea are seen as more masculine if unsweetened (or if too hot to taste). Energy drinks usually avoid this association despite containing hidden sugars and artificial sweeteners. It may also be applied to a few other foods, like lots of chili pepper being regarded as manly. Truth in Television to some extent. One suggested origin of this mindset is the sweet taste of mother's milk and its association with infancy and being looked after. Scientific proof also suggests that children naturally prefer sugar , while adults often grow out of excessively sweet foods and drinks. Contrast with Sweet Tooth and Girls Love Chocolate . See also Genius Sweet Tooth for men who need sugar for brain power, and Donut Mess with a Cop for men in law enforcement needing sugar for those long stakeouts and arduous paperwork. Men who do like sugar are likely to be subject to Sissy Shaming by people who subscribe to this belief. Subtrope to Age-Stereotypical Food and Drink-Based Characterization . See also Real Men Eat Meat . Has nothing to do with Hitler Ate Sugar .
